parameters.name: book.name
parameters.description:
The resource name of the book.

Format: `publishers/{publisher}/books/{book}`

Example: `publishers/1257894000000000000/books/my-book`

parameters.name: updateMask
parameters.description: The list of fields to be updated.

parameters.name: allowMissing
parameters.description:
If set to true, and the book is not found, a new book will be created.
In this situation, `update_mask` is ignored.

200.description:
An example resource type from AIP-123 used to test the behavior described in
the CreateBookRequest message.

See: https://google.aip.dev/123

parameters.name: parent
parameters.description:
The publisher in which to create the book.

Format: `publishers/{publisher}`

Example: `publishers/1257894000000000000`

parameters.name: bookId
parameters.description:
The ID to use for the book.

This must start with an alphanumeric character.

200.description:
An example resource type from AIP-123 used to test the behavior described in
the CreateBookRequest message.

See: https://google.aip.dev/123

parameters.name: updateMask
parameters.description: The paths to update.

parameters.description: The paths to update.

Wrapper message for `string`.

The JSON representation for `StringValue` is JSON string.

@type.description: A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized protocol buffer message.

google.rpc.Status.description: The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for different programming environments.

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

details.description: A list of messages that carry the error details.

@type.description: A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized protocol buffer message.

message.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English.

tags.name: ABitOfEverythingService
tags.description:
ABitOfEverything service is used to validate that APIs with complicated
proto messages and URL templates are still processed correctly.

tags.name: camelCaseServiceName
tags.description: camelCase and lowercase service names are valid but not recommended (use TitleCase instead)
